breakdown:
1. Naming (70/100): Minor improvements replacing timezone.utc with UTC. Documentation uses descriptive headings. Some generic names (data, timestamp) but standard. No outstanding naming issues.

2. Error Handling (50/100): No error handling changes in this PR. The datetime refactoring doesn't affect error handling patterns. Documentation identifies some error handling gaps but doesn't fix them.

3. Type Safety (70/100): The datetime changes (timezone.utc → UTC) are type-equivalent. No new any types introduced. Maintains existing type safety level. No TypedDict/dataclass changes.

4. Architecture (60/100): No architectural changes in the diff itself. Documentation identifies 10 architectural concerns (circular injection, dead imports, future facades) but the actual code changes are trivial import alias updates only.

5. Test Coverage (30/100): No test files modified in this diff. The PR adds documentation but no test coverage for the datetime changes or documented issues.

6. Documentation (90/100): Strong documentation focus. Adds comprehensive summary table with 10 issues, severity ratings (Critical/Moderate/Minor), work items, and action items. Updates status appropriately. Well-structured for a review document.
key_changes: Replaced datetime.now(timezone.utc) with datetime.now(UTC) across 9 locations in base_test.py
Added summary table documenting 10 issues found during review
Documented critical issues: xp schema migration pending, dead D&D imports, inject_modal_state facade needed
Documented moderate issues: undocumented polling path, purity test bug, missing observability section
Documented minor issues: flag semantics, status overclaims, missing PR references, verification gaps
Added duplicate injection issue (#12) to summary table with work item for deduplication
